9.reason: The function per is called with hardcoded arguments (1, 1) instead of reading N from input and passing it as the first argument. This means the program always generates permutations for N=1, ignoring the actual input.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  The function per is called with hardcoded arguments (1, 1) instead of reading N from input and passing it as the first argument. This means the program always generates permutations for N=1, ignoring the actual input.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  The function per is called with hardcoded arguments 1 and 1, but the problem requires reading N from input. This will always generate permutations for N=1 instead of the input N.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The program does not read N from input; it hardcodes N=1, so it will always output permutation of 1 instead of the input N.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  Error: The program does not read the input N. It should first read N from stdin and then call per(n, 1).
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The program fails to read the input integer N. It should use scanf("%d", &n) to read N from standard input and call per(n, 1) instead of hardcoding per(1, 1).
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The program fails to read the input integer N from stdin. It should declare an int variable, read N using scanf, and pass it to per instead of hardcoding 1.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The function call uses hardcoded value 1 instead of reading input N; it should read N from stdin and pass it to per(N, 1)]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The function call uses hardcoded value 1 instead of reading input N; should read N from stdin and pass it to per(N, 1)]
